title: Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for High School and University Students Worldwide
summary: The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is an exciting international academic competition designed to prepare the next generation of scientists, innovators, and critical thinkers for a rapidly advancing quantum future. Open to both high school and university students worldwide, this prestigious challenge provides a unique platform to test knowledge, develop analytical skills, and engage deeply with one of the most transformative fields in modern science: quantum mechanics. As quantum technologies continue to reshape industries ;from computing and cryptography to healthcare and materials science ;opportunities like this challenge serve as vital stepping stones for young minds aiming to make meaningful contributions in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). Overview of the Competition Structure The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is structured into three rigorous and intellectually stimulating rounds: the Qualification Round, Semi-Final Round, and Final Exam. Each phase is carefully designed to progressively deepen participants’ understanding of quantum mechanics while testing both theoretical knowledge and practical problem-solving abilities. Qualification Round (Ongoing) The Qualification Round is currently open and accessible to all eligible students across the globe. Participants are required to solve five quantum mechanics problems independently from home. These problems are a mix of conceptual questions and calculation-based challenges, covering fundamental topics in quantum physics. Each problem carries 5 points, and participants must achieve a minimum score ;12, 14, or 17 points depending on their age group ;to advance to the Semi-Final Round. This flexible scoring system ensures fairness across varying educational levels. Importantly, all participants receive official participation certificates, recognizing their effort and engagement. Additionally, those who submit digitally typed solutions may receive special notes, highlighting the quality and clarity of their work. Semi-Final Round (July 2026) Students who successfully qualify proceed to the Semi-Final Round, which introduces a more advanced level of academic rigor. Participants will engage with real research papers in quantum physics, allowing them to experience how theoretical concepts are applied in actual scientific studies. This stage consists of a supervised 60-minute examination. All necessary materials will be provided via email and participant accounts on Friday, 24 July 2026. A registration fee of 12 EUR applies for this round; however, financial aid is available to support students from socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds, ensuring inclusivity and accessibility. Final Exam (25 August 2026) The Final Round represents the pinnacle of the competition. Scheduled for Tuesday, 25 August 2026, this globally coordinated exam challenges participants with approximately 30 questions in various formats, including multiple-choice and free-response problems. Participants are expected to demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of quantum mechanics, drawing from both foundational principles and insights gained in earlier rounds. Why Participate in the Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge? Participation in this challenge offers far-reaching benefits beyond the competition itself. Academic Growth and Knowledge Expansion Students will explore key quantum concepts such as wave mechanics, quantum tunneling, and spin dynamics. Exposure to real scientific literature in the Semi-Final Round further prepares participants for advanced academic and research pursuits. Development of Critical Thinking and Creativity The competition features original problems crafted by domain experts, encouraging participants to think innovatively and approach challenges from multiple perspectives. This nurtures creativity and sharpens analytical reasoning—skills that are essential in any scientific career. Global Community and Networking Participants become part of a vibrant global network of like-minded students who share a passion for quantum science. This sense of community fosters collaboration, inspiration, and long-term academic connections. Recognition, Awards, and Prizes The competition boasts a total prize pool exceeding $1,500 USD. Awards include International Gold (top 5%), Silver (top 15%), and Bronze (top 30%) medals for each age group. Additional recognitions include National Awards for top performers in each country, as well as special Ambassador, Teacher, and School Awards. All participants, regardless of performance, receive official certificates acknowledging their participation and achievement. Additional Opportunities for Engagement The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge extends beyond student participation by offering roles for teachers and student leaders. Teachers can register to supervise students, access teaching materials, monitor progress, and qualify for Teacher Awards. Schools can also partner with the program to facilitate group participation. Student leaders have the opportunity to become ambassadors, promoting the competition within their schools or regions. Ambassadors receive official certification, recognition on global platforms, and priority consideration for financial aid. The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is more than a competition; it is a transformative academic journey that equips students with the knowledge, skills, and global exposure needed to thrive in the future of science and technology. With its inclusive structure, valuable rewards, and emphasis on real-world application, it stands as a premier opportunity for any student interested in exploring the fascinating world of quantum mechanics. Students are encouraged to take advantage of this opportunity and submit their solutions before the deadline to begin their journey into the quantum future. Deadline: Sunday, 14 June 2026, 23:59 UTC+0 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here . The post Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for High School and University Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027: A Transformative Policy Fellowship for Future Leaders in Mental Health and Technology
summary: The YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027 presents a unique and intellectually rigorous opportunity for students and recent graduates who are passionate about influencing policy, advancing mental health systems, and exploring the intersection of technology and social impact. Designed as a year-long academic fellowship running from Fall 2026 through Spring 2027, this program empowers participants to transition from theoretical classroom learning into practical, high-impact policy research. In an era where artificial intelligence, digital systems, and healthcare delivery are rapidly evolving, this fellowship stands out as a forward-thinking initiative that equips fellows with the tools, mentorship, and collaborative environment necessary to address real-world challenges affecting underserved and vulnerable populations. Program Overview The YIM Macro Research Fellowship is structured to provide fellows with hands-on experience in policy research, analysis, and advocacy. Participants are grouped into collaborative cohorts, where they engage deeply with one of three cutting-edge research tracks that reflect pressing global concerns: 1. AI Mental Health Access This track explores how artificial intelligence, particularly AI-powered chatbots, can be ethically deployed to expand access to mental health services for low-income populations. Fellows will examine both the opportunities and ethical dilemmas associated with integrating AI into mental health care systems, including concerns around bias, accuracy, and human oversight. 2. Digital Surveillance and Data Privacy In an increasingly digitized healthcare environment, protecting client data has become a critical issue. This track focuses on safeguarding sensitive mental health information, addressing surveillance risks, and developing policies that ensure privacy and ethical data use in digital health systems. 3. The Digital Divide and AI-Augmented Telehealth This research area investigates disparities in access to digital healthcare technologies. Fellows will analyze how AI-enhanced telehealth solutions can bridge or widen the gap for underserved communities, particularly those with limited access to internet infrastructure or digital literacy. What Fellows Will Gain Participants in the YIM Macro Research Fellowship will benefit from a comprehensive and immersive learning experience that goes far beyond traditional academic settings: Original Policy Research: Fellows will develop and produce a well-researched, original policy brief addressing systemic issues within their chosen track. This work serves as a tangible output that can be used for academic, professional, or advocacy purposes. Monthly Mentorship: Each fellow is paired with an experienced macro social work professional who provides guidance, feedback, and insights throughout the fellowship. This mentorship fosters both academic growth and professional development. Collaborative Learning Environment: Fellows work in teams, encouraging peer-to-peer learning, diverse perspectives, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Capstone Presentation in Washington, DC: At the conclusion of the fellowship, participants have the opportunity to present their research in Washington, DC, gaining exposure to policy professionals and stakeholders. Professional Networking: The program facilitates connections with a broader network of like-minded individuals, mentors, and professionals in the fields of social work, public policy, and digital health. Why This Fellowship Matters The YIM Macro Research Fellowship is not just an academic exercise—it is a platform for real-world impact. Fellows engage with systemic issues that affect real communities, particularly those who are often marginalized or underserved. By focusing on mental health and technology, the program addresses one of the most urgent and evolving areas in global health and policy. Participants leave the fellowship not only with enhanced research and writing skills but also with a deeper understanding of policy development, ethical considerations in technology, and the practical challenges of implementing change in complex systems. Who Should Apply This fellowship is ideal for individuals who are intellectually curious, socially conscious, and eager to contribute to meaningful policy discussions. Applicants should demonstrate: A strong interest in mental health, public policy, and technological innovation The ability to think critically and engage with complex ethical issues Effective writing and communication skills A willingness to collaborate and contribute to a team-based research environment Students and recent graduates from social sciences and related fields are especially encouraged to apply. Application Deadline The specific application deadline has not been stated. Interested applicants are advised to apply early once the application portal opens to increase their chances of selection. title: Fully Fund Your Education: Native American Scholarship Program 2026–2027 by New Mexico Gas Company
summary: Pursuing higher education is a journey that demands persistence, sacrifice, and a deep commitment to personal growth. For many students, especially those balancing financial challenges, accessing quality education can feel like a distant goal. The Native American Scholarship Program (NASP), established by the New Mexico Gas Company (NMGC), is designed to bridge that gap by providing meaningful financial support to Native American students across New Mexico. This scholarship program stands out not only for its financial value but also for its inclusive and flexible eligibility criteria, making it accessible to a wide range of students pursuing different educational paths. About the Native American Scholarship Program (NASP) The Native American Scholarship Program (NASP) is an initiative aimed at supporting Native American students in New Mexico who are committed to advancing their education. Since its inception in 2011, the program has awarded over $524,000 to 265 students representing 20 of the 23 Pueblos, Tribes, and Nations in New Mexico. The program reflects NMGC’s commitment to empowering Indigenous communities through education by investing in students who demonstrate motivation and a clear desire to build their futures through learning. What Makes NASP Unique? Unlike many traditional scholarship programs, NASP takes a more inclusive and student-centered approach. While most scholarships emphasize strict academic metrics such as high GPAs, recommendation letters, or specific majors, NASP removes these barriers to create broader access. Key distinguishing features include: No minimum GPA requirement No letters of recommendation required Open to all fields of study Accepts both full-time and part-time students Includes trade schools and technical training programs This flexibility ensures that students from diverse academic and professional interests have equal opportunities to benefit. Scholarship Value and Awards For the 2026–2027 academic year: A total of 20 scholarships will be awarded Each scholarship is valued at $3,000 Both new applicants and renewal applicants are eligible Students can apply for renewal annually (up to three consecutive renewals) The number of new scholarships awarded each year may vary depending on how many previous recipients apply for renewal. Scholarship funds are paid directly to recipients to support educational expenses such as tuition, materials, and other academic costs. Application Timeline Applicants should take note of the official application window: Applications Open: March 30, 2026 Notification of Results: Late June 2026 Successful applicants will be recognized at the Native American Scholarship Program Awards Banquet, where scholarship checks will be distributed. Those unable to attend will receive their awards by mail. Who Can Apply? The NASP is specifically designed for Native American students who meet the following criteria: Must be a resident of New Mexico Must be an enrolled member of a New Mexico Pueblo, Tribe, or Nation Must be: A high school senior graduating soon, or A GED holder, or A current post-secondary student Eligible students can be enrolled in: Universities or colleges (associate, bachelor’s, or master’s degrees) Trade schools Technical or specialized training programs Importantly, students are free to pursue any field of study, making this program adaptable to a wide range of career aspirations. Application and Selection Process Applications are submitted online through the official NMGC-NASP platform. Each application is reviewed by the NASP Committee, which is responsible for evaluating submissions and awarding scholarships. For renewal applicants, additional documentation may be required, including: Proof of continued enrollment An essay outlining academic progress The program also verifies tribal enrollment status through the applicant’s respective tribal government. Responsibilities of Award Recipients Students who receive the scholarship are encouraged to: Provide updates on their academic progress during the year Maintain good standing in their educational institution Submit renewal applications if seeking continued support Additionally, recipients may have their names and photographs published by NMGC for promotional purposes, as part of the program’s outreach and impact visibility. Why This Scholarship Matters The NASP is more than just financial aid; it is an investment in the future of Native American communities. By supporting students across various educational pathways, including technical and vocational training, the program acknowledges the evolving demands of today’s workforce. It empowers students not only to achieve academic success but also to return to their communities equipped with skills and knowledge that can drive development and innovation. The Native American Scholarship Program is a powerful opportunity for eligible students to pursue their educational dreams without the burden of financial strain. Its inclusive design, flexible requirements, and commitment to community empowerment make it one of the most impactful scholarship programs available to Native American students in New Mexico. If you meet the eligibility criteria, this is your chance to take a significant step toward achieving your academic and career goals. Deadline : May 1 ,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here . title: Program Coordinator Job in the Philippines (Remote) – Join The Max Foundation and Drive Global Health Equity
summary: If you are passionate about healthcare delivery, patient advocacy, and global impact, this opportunity with The Max Foundation could be your next big step. With over 26 years of experience in advancing access to life-saving treatments, The Max Foundation is offering an entry-level Program Coordinator role based in Manila, Philippines (remote/home office), designed for individuals eager to contribute to meaningful healthcare change. About The Max Foundation The Max Foundation is a globally recognized nonprofit organization committed to accelerating health equity. Operating across low- and middle-income countries, the organization has positively impacted more than 100,000 patients living with cancer and other critical illnesses. Their mission is rooted in the belief that geography should never determine access to life-saving medicines or quality healthcare. Through partnerships with healthcare providers, patient organizations, and global stakeholders, The Max Foundation continues to develop scalable, patient-centered healthcare solutions that bring hope and dignity to underserved populations. Role Overview: Program Coordinator The Program Coordinator (PC) plays a vital role in managing the day-to-day operations of The Max Foundation’s programs in the Philippines. Reporting directly to the Program Officer, this contractor position involves a mix of administrative coordination, patient engagement, logistics management, and stakeholder collaboration. This is not just an administrative role, it is a frontline position where you will directly impact patients’ lives by ensuring access to treatment, care, and support systems. Key Responsibilities As a Program Coordinator, you will be responsible for a wide range of duties that ensure smooth program implementation and patient support: Program Operations Administration You will oversee the daily functioning of support programs, ensuring that all activities align with established organizational guidelines. This includes managing documentation, coordinating logistics, and maintaining operational efficiency. Patient Support Engagement A critical part of your role involves working directly with patients. You will assist in enrolling eligible patients into programs, ensure compliance, and provide emotional support and basic disease education when necessary. Collaboration with Healthcare Professionals You will work closely with physicians, nurses, pharmacists, and other healthcare stakeholders. This collaboration ensures that patients receive timely access to medication and appropriate care. Logistics Supply Chain Coordination The role includes supporting the importation and distribution of donated medicines. You will assist clinics in navigating customs clearance and ensuring that life-saving treatments reach patients without delay. Data Management Reporting Maintaining accurate and up-to-date database records is essential. You will also report adverse events within 24 hours, ensuring compliance with safety and program protocols. Advocacy Storytelling You will help amplify patient voices by collecting and sharing their stories. These narratives play a powerful role in advocacy and awareness campaigns. Stakeholder Communication Clear and professional communication with patients, healthcare professionals, and partner organizations is crucial. You will also provide regular updates to the Program Officer and support reporting processes. Required Qualifications To succeed in this role, candidates should meet the following criteria: A diploma is required; a bachelor’s degree in Social Work, Public Health, Nursing, or a related field is preferred At least one year of experience in a healthcare-related field Experience in medicine management or supply chain is an added advantage Strong communication skills to engage effectively with diverse stakeholders Fluency in English and Filipino Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Strong organizational and documentation skills Ability to work independently and manage tasks efficiently Willingness to work weekends when necessary Must possess personal transportation Skills and Attributes for Success The ideal candidate is not just qualified but deeply aligned with The Max Foundation’s mission. You should demonstrate: A strong passion for global health equity and patient-centered care High attention to detail and commitment to quality work Adaptability in a fast-paced, high-demand environment Strong interpersonal and collaboration skills A compassionate, people-first mindset Resourcefulness and problem-solving ability Work Structure and Compensation This is a contractor position based in Manila, Philippines, operating from a home office. Compensation is competitive and commensurate with experience. Please note that relocation support is not provided. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply directly through The Max Foundation’s official careers page. Ensure that your application includes both a résumé and a well-crafted cover letter highlighting your experience and motivation. Why This Opportunity Matters This role is more than a job, it is an opportunity to become part of a global movement dedicated to saving lives and improving healthcare access. By joining The Max Foundation, you will contribute to a mission that transcends borders and transforms lives every day. Deadline : Not specified , apply ASAP. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. title: Grocers’ Charity Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity for UK Charities Supporting Communities, Health, and Social Impact
summary: The Grocer’s Grant is a highly impactful funding opportunity designed to empower small UK-registered charities working to improve lives, strengthen communities, and address pressing social challenges. With an annual funding pool of approximately £1 million, this grant provides one-off financial support to charities that are making a tangible difference across a broad range of sectors. If your organization is committed to social good; whether through supporting vulnerable populations, promoting health and well being, or preserving the environment; this grant offers a meaningful opportunity to secure funding and expand your impact. Overview of the Grocers’ Charity Grant The Grocers’ Charity provides one-off grants typically up to £5,000, although larger amounts may be awarded in exceptional cases where projects demonstrate significant need and potential impact. The funding is allocated at the discretion of the charity’s trustees, who carefully review each application to ensure alignment with the foundation’s mission and priorities. The grant supports projects across several key areas of social intervention, ensuring a wide range of charitable initiatives can benefit. Funding Focus Areas The Grocers’ Charity Grant supports charities working in the following sectors: 1. Relief of Hardship Projects that aim to alleviate poverty and improve living conditions are strongly encouraged. This includes initiatives providing training, improved facilities, and support for individuals at risk of homelessness or domestic abuse, particularly in high-deprivation areas. 2. Children and Young People (0–25 years) Funding is available for programs that empower young people to reach their full potential. This includes initiatives focused on personal development, wellbeing, peer support, and group engagement activities. 3. The Elderly Charities working to combat loneliness and social isolation among older adults are eligible. Programs may include befriending services, social inclusion initiatives, and support to enhance daily living. 4. Disability and Inclusion Organizations that identify and remove barriers to participation for people with disabilities can apply. This includes frontline services that promote accessibility, inclusion, and equal opportunities. 5. Health Health-focused charities can receive funding for ethical medical research, procurement of essential equipment, and initiatives supporting individuals with physical or mental health conditions. Note that health and medical charities may have a higher income threshold. 6. Military Projects that support current and former service personnel and their families are eligible. This includes programs focused on employment, education, and overall wellbeing. 7. The Arts The grant encourages artistic development and cultural engagement, especially for marginalized communities. This includes supporting artists with financial challenges or disabilities and promoting access to arts for underserved audiences. 8. Heritage Funding is available for preserving historical assets, including restoring buildings (excluding places of worship) and conserving important objects or artworks. 9. Environment and Conservation Charities working on environmental sustainability, biodiversity protection, and climate change mitigation can benefit. Projects may include habitat restoration, environmental education, and initiatives to reduce pollution and waste. Grant Value and Financial Scope Typical Grant Size: Up to £5,000 Exceptional Funding: Larger grants may be awarded for high-impact projects Annual Funding Pool: Approximately £1 million This funding can be used for tangible, project-based expenses that directly contribute to the charity’s mission and objectives. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Grocers’ Charity Grant, applicants must: Be a UK-registered charity Have an annual turnover below £500,000 For health and medical charities, have an income below £15 million Deliver projects that directly benefit communities within the UK Restrictions The following are not eligible to apply: Places of worship Educational institutions such as schools, colleges, and universities Individuals Non-UK registered organizations (including CICs) Charities whose beneficiaries are located outside the UK Charities exceeding the turnover threshold (except eligible medical charities) Application Process The application process is structured in two stages: Stage 1: Initial Enquiry Applicants must complete an online eligibility checklist and submit an Initial Enquiry Form. Responses are typically provided within three weeks after the application deadline. Stage 2: Full Application (By Invitation Only) Successful applicants from Stage 1 will be invited to complete a detailed online application form. They will have one week to submit this form. Deadline The closing date for applications is 1 September 2026. Applicants are advised to begin the process early to ensure adequate time for preparation and submission. Why You Should Apply The Grocers’ Charity Grant is more than just funding; it is an opportunity to scale your impact, strengthen your programs, and reach more beneficiaries. With its broad funding scope and commitment to community development, this grant is ideal for charities looking to make a measurable difference in society. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity , click here For more global opportunities, click here The post Grocers’ Charity Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity for UK Charities Supporting Communities, Health, and Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MMD Grant Facility 2026: Call for Proposals Supporting Migration, Mobility, and Policy Innovation Across Africa and Europe
summary: The second Call for Proposals under the Migration and Mobility Dialogue (MMD) Grant Facility has officially been launched on April 7, 2026 , offering a strategic funding opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of migration, policy, research, and international cooperation . Funded by the Directorate-General for International Partnerships (DG INTPA) under the Africa-EU Migration and Mobility Dialogue Support Programme , this initiative aims to strengthen collaboration between African and European stakeholders while promoting evidence-based policy development and innovative solutions in migration governance. Overview of the MMD Grant Facility The MMD Grant Facility is designed to support short-term, high-impact projects that contribute to: Strengthening migration and mobility dialogues Enhancing policy coherence and cooperation Generating research and knowledge outputs Addressing emerging migration-related challenges This second call builds on the success of previous initiatives and continues to promote cross-regional collaboration between Africa and Europe . Funding Details The total indicative budget allocated for this call is: EUR 600,000 Individual grants will range between: EUR 100,000 and EUR 150,000 Importantly, the program allows: Up to 100% funding of eligible project costs , where properly justified This makes the grant particularly accessible to non-profit organizations and research institutions with limited co-financing capacity. Scope of Eligible Projects Projects submitted under this call must align with the overarching goals of the MMD framework. Specifically, eligible actions should: 1. Support Migration Dialogues Contribute to the operationalization of key migration frameworks, including: Rabat Process Khartoum Process C2CMMD 2. Generate Knowledge and Policy Input Projects should produce: Research outputs Policy recommendations Data-driven insights 3. Align with Strategic Priorities Actions must align with one or more domains of the: Joint Valletta Action Plan Or address transversal themes such as: Climate-induced migration Migration and gender Migration and health Geographic Scope and Implementation Projects must: Be implemented in up to three countries Include at least one African Union Member State Optionally include activities within the EU, particularly when targeting diaspora communities Project Duration Maximum duration: 6 months All projects must be completed by: March 1, 2027 Who Can Apply? The call is open to a wide range of non-profit and non-state actors , including: Civil society organizations (CSOs) Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Diaspora organizations Social enterprises Universities and research centers Lead Applicant Requirements To be eligible, lead applicants must: Be established for at least 2 years Be registered in an African Union (AU) or European Union (EU) Member State Partnership Requirements Applications may include one co-applicant EU-based lead applicants must include an AU-based co-applicant , unless they are diaspora organizations AU-based applicants are not required to include co-applicants Affiliated entities may also be included Important Restriction Organizations that received funding under the first MMD Call for Proposals (as lead or co-applicant) are not eligible for this round. Application Process All applications must be submitted via the official platform managed by the International Centre for Migration Policy Development . Submission platform: ICMPD Electronic Application Platform Each organization may participate in only one application (as lead, co-applicant, or affiliated entity) Deadline Application Deadline: May 1, 2026 at 17:00 (Vienna time) Late submissions will not be considered. Additional Information Information Session An online information session will be held on: April 16, 2026 at 10:00 (Vienna time) The registration link will be available on the application platform. Questions and Clarifications Questions can be submitted via the platform until: April 23, 2026 Annual Retrospective Applicants are encouraged to review the 2025 Annual Retrospective , which highlights key achievements and impact of previously funded projects. title: Becoming a Force of Nature 2026: Global Online Programme Empowering Young Change-Makers in Climate and Social Justice
summary: In a rapidly changing world shaped by climate challenges and social inequalities, young people are increasingly stepping forward as catalysts for transformation. The Force of Nature Becoming a Force of Nature (BaFoN) Programme is a powerful global initiative designed to equip aspiring change makers with the clarity, skills, and confidence needed to turn their passion into meaningful action. This immersive online programme provides a structured yet deeply reflective journey, helping participants bridge the gap between their vision for a better world and the practical steps required to achieve it. If you are a young individual eager to create impact in climate or social justice spaces, this programme offers the tools and community you need to begin. What is the Becoming a Force of Nature Programme? Becoming a Force of Nature (BaFoN) is a three-part online programme tailored for individuals aged 16 to 35. It is delivered four times annually and focuses on guiding participants through a transformational process; from understanding their emotions and motivations to developing actionable solutions for real-world challenges. The programme runs over three weeks, with one session per week. Each session lasts between 90 and 120 minutes, making it flexible and accessible for students, young professionals, and emerging leaders worldwide. Participants are expected to dedicate approximately 2.5 hours per week, which includes live sessions and any reflective or follow-up activities. For those unable to attend live sessions, recordings are made available, ensuring inclusivity and convenience. Programme Structure and Learning Journey The BaFoN programme is thoughtfully divided into three interconnected workshops, each addressing a critical stage in the journey of becoming an effective changemaker. 1. Anxiety to Agency This session explores the emotional and psychological dimensions of climate change and social issues. Participants learn to: Understand the relationship between mental health and global challenges Identify and acknowledge complex emotions such as anxiety, fear, or frustration Build resilience strategies both individually and collectively Apply practical tools to manage emotions and transform them into motivation for action This stage is particularly important in helping participants move from feeling overwhelmed to feeling empowered. 2. Discovery (Agency to Efficacy) In this phase, participants begin to uncover their unique identity as changemakers. The session focuses on: Identifying personal strengths, passions, and talents Clarifying individual values and purpose (“your why”) Understanding what drives fulfillment and effectiveness in social impact work Building a personal framework to guide decision-making and action This stage ensures that participants are not only motivated but also aligned with their authentic selves. 3. Catalyst (Efficacy to Action) The final session is action-oriented and equips participants with the tools to implement their ideas. Participants will: Define and analyze a specific problem they want to address Map out the problem with a focus on affected communities Develop a clear and actionable plan Identify next steps to turn ideas into tangible impact By the end of this stage, participants leave with a concrete roadmap for creating change. Programme Schedule The upcoming May 2026 cohort will take place on: 12th May 2026 19th May 2026 26th May 2026 Each session is conducted online, making it accessible to participants from all parts of the world. Application Process and Deadline To join the Becoming a Force of Nature programme, applicants are required to complete an online application form. Application Deadline: 7th May 2026 at 12:00 PM BST Due to the competitive nature of the programme and its global reach, early application is strongly encouraged. Why You Should Apply The Becoming a Force of Nature programme is not just another online course; it is a transformational experience. Participants gain: A deeper understanding of their role in addressing global challenges Practical tools for managing climate anxiety and building resilience Clarity on personal purpose and direction Skills to design and implement impactful projects Access to a global network of like-minded young changemakers In a time when the world needs bold, thoughtful, and action-driven leaders, this programme provides a strong foundation for anyone ready to step into that role. The Becoming a Force of Nature programme stands out as a unique opportunity for young people to not only learn but also evolve. By combining emotional intelligence, self-discovery, and practical action planning, it ensures that participants are fully equipped to lead change in their communities and beyond. If you are passionate about climate action, social justice, or community impact, this programme could be your next big step toward making a difference. Application Deadline: 7th May 2026 at 12:00 PM BST APPLY HERE For mor information on this opportunity , click here. For more global opportunities, click here . title: Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies 2035: UK Government Survey for Early-Career Academics
summary: As the global technological landscape evolves at an unprecedented pace, governments are increasingly relying on forward-looking research and expert insights to anticipate future disruptions. The Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies Survey , led by the UK Government Office for Science (GO-Science) , represents a unique opportunity for early-career academics based in the United Kingdom to directly contribute to shaping national policy and long-term strategic thinking. This initiative is part of a broader effort to identify emerging technologies, potential risks, and transformative opportunities that could define the world by the year 2035 . What is Horizon Scanning and Why Does It Matter? Horizon scanning is a strategic process used by governments and organizations to: Detect early signals of change Identify emerging technologies and trends Assess future risks and opportunities Support evidence-based policymaking Through this survey, the UK Government aims to gather expert-driven insights from academics who are actively engaged in cutting-edge research and innovation. About the Survey The survey is conducted by the Emerging Technology Team within GO-Science , a body dedicated to analyzing technological developments and informing policy decisions at the highest levels of government. Participants are invited to: Assess up to three emerging technologies within their field Share perspectives on potentially disruptive or unexpected innovations Reflect on how these technologies could evolve and impact society by 2035 All questions are optional , allowing participants flexibility in how they contribute. Why Participate? This survey offers a valuable opportunity to: Influence UK government policy and strategic planning Contribute to national and global conversations on technology Share your expertise on future innovations and disruptions Participate in a confidential and anonymous research initiative Your insights will play a critical role in shaping how governments prepare for technological change. Participation Details Participation in the survey is entirely voluntary . Respondents can: Choose which questions to answer Exit the survey at any time before submission Submit responses only when comfortable By completing and submitting the survey, participants confirm their consent to take part under the outlined conditions. Data Privacy and Anonymity A key feature of this initiative is its strong emphasis on privacy and ethical data handling : The survey is designed not to collect personal data Responses are fully anonymous Participants are advised not to include identifiable information Any accidental personal data will be removed before analysis This ensures that all contributions remain secure and confidential. How Your Responses Will Be Used The collected data will be analyzed in aggregate to: Identify key technological trends and themes Support government briefings and internal analysis Inform policy development and strategic decisions Importantly: Individual responses will not be attributed Any external publications will present high-level, non-identifiable insights Deadline No specific deadline has been publicly stated. Participants are encouraged to complete the survey as soon as possible to ensure their insights are included in the analysis. title: R2I Fellowship 2026 at Wale University: Bridging Africa’s Research Gap Through Innovation and Real-World Impact
summary: Africa stands at a critical crossroads where innovation, research, and real-world application must converge to unlock the continent’s full potential. At the forefront of this transformation is Wale University , through its dynamic initiative— Wale Lab Innovation —which is redefining how research translates into tangible impact. The R2I Fellowship 2026 (Research to Impact Fellowship) is now open, offering a unique opportunity for aspiring researchers and innovators across Africa to transform their ideas into real-world solutions. The Vision Behind Wale Lab Innovation Launched on February 2nd, 2025 , Wale Lab operates as a key arm of Wale University with a bold mission: to reverse Africa’s research deficit and empower a new generation of innovators capable of driving sustainable development. Unlike traditional academic frameworks, Wale Lab focuses on applied research , ensuring that ideas do not remain confined to theory but are developed into actionable, impactful solutions. Understanding the Research Gap in Africa Despite its vast human capital and intellectual potential, Africa faces significant structural challenges in research and innovation: 1. Population vs Research Output Gap Africa represents 18% of the global population , yet contributes less than 1% of global research output . This imbalance highlights systemic barriers in funding, infrastructure, and access to knowledge platforms. 2. Collaboration Access Gap Over 70% of African researchers lack access to global collaboration opportunities , limiting exposure, partnerships, and knowledge exchange essential for innovation. 3. Research Implementation Gap Nearly 90% of research ideas never reach real-world implementation , meaning valuable insights and solutions fail to create societal impact. How Wale Lab is Changing the Narrative Wale Lab is actively addressing these challenges by: Connecting African researchers with global opportunities and networks Providing expert mentorship from experienced professionals Supporting the transition from research to implementation Encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration across 22+ research areas Through this model, Wale Lab ensures that innovation is not only created but also scaled and applied . The R2I Fellowship: From Research to Impact The R2I Fellowship (Research to Impact) is a flagship program under Wale Lab designed to equip participants with the tools, guidance, and resources needed to turn research ideas into actionable solutions. What Makes This Fellowship Unique? The program stands out due to its strong emphasis on: Real-world application of research Mentorship-driven development Global collaboration opportunities Hands-on experience in innovation ecosystems Participants are not only trained but also supported in producing outputs that have measurable societal and economic impact. Program Reach and Impact Since its inception, Wale Lab has demonstrated remarkable growth and engagement: 2,500+ applications received for its inaugural cohort Participation from 9+ countries , showcasing strong regional diversity Support from 46+ expert mentors Development of 36+ research manuscripts ready for implementation This momentum reflects the urgent need and strong demand for platforms that bridge the gap between research and impact in Africa . Who Should Apply? The R2I Fellowship is ideal for: Students and recent graduates Early-career researchers Innovators and problem-solvers Individuals passionate about Africa’s development through research and innovation Applicants should have a clear interest in transforming ideas into practical solutions that address real-world challenges. Benefits of the R2I Fellowship Participants in the fellowship will gain: Access to expert mentorship and guidance Opportunities for global collaboration Support in developing research into implementable solutions Exposure to innovation-driven ecosystems The ability to contribute to Africa’s socio-economic transformation How to Apply Interested candidates are encouraged to apply through the official Wale Lab platform. Access the application portal Review program details Submit your application Deadline Application Deadline: May 3, 2026 Applicants are strongly advised to apply early to maximize their chances of selection. title: REACT 2.0 Digital Innovation Fund for Energy and Climate (DIFEC): Funding Opportunity for Early-Stage Digital Climate Solutions
summary: The REACT 2.0 Digital Innovation Fund for Energy and Climate (DIFEC) represents a transformative opportunity for startups and enterprises operating at the intersection of digital innovation, climate action, and decentralized renewable energy (DRE) . Designed to accelerate impactful solutions, this fund specifically targets early-stage companies that are building digital tools to address pressing energy and climate challenges. What is the DIFEC Growing Digital Innovation Funding Window? The Window 1: Growing Digital Innovation Funding Window is a dedicated funding stream under the REACT 2.0 program. Its primary objective is to de-risk early-stage digital solutions by supporting innovators during one of the most critical phases of their development journey. At this stage, many promising solutions struggle due to limited financial resources, insufficient market validation, or lack of technical infrastructure. DIFEC directly addresses these gaps by offering both financial and technical support to ensure that solutions can evolve into scalable and sustainable business models. Key Objectives of the Program The Growing Window focuses on: Supporting product development and refinement Enabling market validation and testing Helping startups achieve product–market fit Strengthening digital risk awareness Promoting secure-by-design development practices Encouraging responsible data governance and usage As digital technologies become increasingly integrated into climate and energy systems, the fund emphasizes the importance of cybersecurity, data protection, and ethical digital practices . Who is the Fund Targeting? The DIFEC Growing Window is specifically designed for: Early-stage companies and startups Enterprises working on digital solutions for climate and energy sectors Innovators integrating technology into decentralized renewable energy systems These may include platforms, tools, or systems that improve energy access, optimize energy consumption, enhance climate monitoring, or support sustainability initiatives through digital innovation. Financing Instrument and Ticket Size The fund provides grant-based financing , aimed at reducing financial risk for early-stage innovators. While the exact ticket size may vary depending on the project scope and needs, the funding is structured to provide meaningful support for development and validation phases. Type of Support Available Beyond financial assistance, selected companies benefit from a holistic support package that may include: Technical assistance and mentorship Capacity-building in digital security and compliance Strategic guidance for scaling and market entry Support in strengthening internal processes and governance Desired Socio-Economic Impact The DIFEC program seeks to generate measurable and sustainable impact, including: Increased access to clean and affordable energy Enhanced climate resilience and adaptation Promotion of digital transformation in green sectors Job creation and economic growth within sustainable industries Matching Contributions Applicants may be required to demonstrate co-financing or matching contributions , ensuring commitment and shared investment in the success of the project. Eligibility Criteria To be considered, applicants must: Be a registered company or enterprise Operate within the energy and climate innovation ecosystem Demonstrate a clear digital component in their solution Be at an early stage of development , seeking support for scaling and validation Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Innovation and uniqueness of the solution Feasibility and scalability Potential socio-economic and environmental impact Team capacity and execution capability Alignment with program priorities Investment Principles and Additionality The fund follows strong investment principles, ensuring that: Funding creates additional value beyond what is available in the market Projects demonstrate clear need for support Investments contribute to long-term sustainability and resilience Commitments from Successful Grantees Selected applicants are expected to: Actively participate in program activities Adhere to responsible digital practices Deliver on agreed milestones and reporting requirements Contribute to knowledge sharing and ecosystem development How to Apply Interested applicants can access detailed documentation and submit their applications through the official resources below: Program Brief FAQs Term Sheet Application Portal Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review all documents before submitting their proposals. Deadline Applicants should check the official application portal and documentation for the exact submission deadline , as timelines may vary depending on the funding cycle.
